DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF JOYCE EDWARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS IS DENIED. THIS MOTION IS NOT PROPERLY BROUGHT UNDER THE SUNSHINE ORDINANCE. THE MOTION IS NOT TIMELY BROUGHT AS A MOTION PURSUANT TO THE DISCOVERY STATUTES AS THE TIME TO BRING THE MOTION HAD EXPIRED AT THE TIME THE MOTION WAS FILED. THE COURT SUGGESTS TO THE CITY ATTORNEY THAT THE RESPONSE BE AMENDED TO INCLUDE THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THE OPPOSITION REGARDING THE REASONS THAT THE VIDEO TAPE DOES NOT EXIST. Notice And Motion To Compel Response To Production Of Documents And Things
Off Calendar May-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. DANIEL A. ZAHEER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3822 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CCSF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

PRULING- THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - Plaintiff JOYCE EDWARD'S Motion To Compel Response To Production Of Documents And Things is denied. This motion is not properly brought under the Sunshine Ordinance. The motion is not timely brought as a motion pursuant to the discovery statutes as the time to bring the motion had expired at the time the motion was filed. The court suggests to the City Attorney that the response be amended to include the information contained in the opposition regarding the reasons that the video tape does not exist. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR.
